mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the target horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off capital flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases, indicating demand proxies are decoupled from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs higher financing costs, preventing earnings downgrades and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, breaking the supply-resilience mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, refuting the inflation-risk premium channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment confirms a healthy labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, indicating labor dynamics are irrelevant to equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with corporate earnings expansion, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year period, invalidating the earnings-growth transmission.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, prompting equity market sell-offs and negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, breaking the supply-contraction sell-off mechanism.
